title: The formation of magnetic reentrancy in the Ising model on a decorated square lattice
authors: A. V. Zarubin, F. A. Kassan-Ogly
abstract: In our work, based on an exact solution of the Ising model on a decorated square lattice with an arbitrary number of decorating spins, we have demonstrated the fundamental possibility of describing the phenomenon of multiple magnetic phase transitions (magnetic reentrancy) in the regime of competing exchange interactions. We analyzed the magnetic behavior of the system and established the conditions for the occurrence of magnetic reentrancy. We have determined the relationships of the model parameter under which the formation of one, three, and even five magnetic phase transitions is possible, which is confirmed by a complicated magnetic phase diagram. We have also proposed a unique method for finding critical temperatures that allows for the precise determination of their number and values, including those at extremely low temperatures.
summary: This work provides an exact analytical solution for the Ising model on a decorated square lattice to study magnetic reentrancy. By analyzing how multiple, competing exchange interactions influence the system's energy landscape, the authors demonstrate that the material can exhibit up to five distinct magnetic phase transitions as temperature changes. This is significant for understanding complex ordering phenomena in frustrated quantum magnets.
main_problem: The study investigates the fundamental possibility of multiple magnetic phase transitions, known as magnetic reentrancy, in spin systems subjected to competing exchange interactions.
main_result: The model demonstrates that up to five distinct magnetic phase transitions can occur under specific conditions, which are governed by the interplay between direct and decorating exchange interactions.
method: The analysis relies on an exact solution for the Ising model derived via a decoration-iteration transformation, determining critical temperatures by solving algebraic equations related to the principal eigenvalue of the transfer matrix.
model_or_system: The system is the Ising model defined on a decorated square lattice, incorporating direct exchange interactions ($J_x, J_y$) and decorating exchange interactions ($J_{dx}, J_{dy}$), allowing for arbitrary decoration multiplicities.
key_observables: Spontaneous magnetization ($M$), critical temperatures ($T_c$), and the identification of distinct magnetic ordering phases (e.g., F–F, AF–AF).
important_parameters: The relative strengths and signs of direct vs. decorating exchange interactions ($J$ vs $J_d$) are crucial; reentrancy occurs when $|J_d| > |J|$ under certain conditions.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the Boltzmann constant is unity, and computational methods are used to rigorously find roots for critical temperatures, bypassing difficulties near low-temperature divergences.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate magnetic phase diagrams showing $T_c$ dependence on interaction parameters ($J_d$, $J_{dx}$), demonstrating transitions ranging from one up to five distinct points under isotropic and anisotropic conditions.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the model by first establishing the mathematical framework using exact solutions, then deriving parameter conditions for competing interactions, and finally mapping out complex magnetic phase diagrams showing the emergence of multiple transition points based on anisotropy.
why_interesting: While focused on classical statistical mechanics, the concept of sequential, multi-stage ordering transitions (reentrancy) due to competing interactions is highly relevant to understanding complex ground states in quantum many-body systems and frustrated magnets.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Example of a decorated square lattice: a lattice decorated twice in two directions (dx = dy = 2). Blue circles represent nodal spins; red diamonds represent decorating spins. Blue dashed lines represent direct exchange interactions with corresponding values Jx and Jy along the x and y directions of the lattice; red dotted lines rep- resent decorating exchange interactions with corresponding values Jdx and Jdy. The black solid lines show the spatial lattice on which the atomic spins of the lattice are located

Figure 2. Magnetic phase diagrams showing the dependence of the critical temperature Tc on the values of the decorating exchange in- teraction Jd at J = −1, d = 5, Jd < 0 (a), J = +1, d = 6, Jd < 0 (b), J = −1, d = 5, Jd > 0 (c), J = −1, d = 6, Jd > 0 (d). Magnetic ordered phases are labeled in the figure as F–F (ferromagnetic-ferromagnetic) and AF–AF (antiferromagnetic- antiferromagnetic), and the paramagnetic phase as P

Figure 4. Magnetic phase diagrams showing the dependence of the critical temperature Tc on the decorating exchange interaction Jd (a) and on the decoration multiplicity d (b) of the lattice for the set of parameters (23). The red dots indicate the critical temperatures Tc1 ≈ 0.141, Tc2 ≈0.376, Tc3 ≈2.234 at Jd = +1.2 (a) and at d = 6 (b). The temperature dependence of the spontaneous magnetization (c) for the parameters (23) is shown by the red solid line, while the Onsager’s spontaneous magnetization (1) with Tc ≈2.269 is shown by the blue dashed line

Figure 3. Magnetic phase diagrams showing the dependence of the critical temperature Tc on the decorating exchange interaction Jd (a) and on the decoration multiplicity d (b) of the lattice for the set of parameters (22). The red dot indicates the critical temperature Tc1 ≈ 2.258 at Jd = +1 (a) and at d = 6 (b). The temperature dependence of the spontaneous magnetization (c) for the parameters (22) is shown by the red solid line, while the Onsager’s spontaneous magnetization (1) with Tc ≈2.269 is shown by the blue dashed line

Figure 5. Magnetic phase diagrams showing the dependence of the critical temperature Tc on the decorating exchange interaction Jdx (Jdy = Jdx + 0.3) (a) and the decoration multiplicity dx (dy = dx) (b) of the lattice for the set of parameters (24). The red dots denote the critical temperatures Tc1 ≈0.197, Tc2 ≈0.224, Tc3 ≈0.338, Tc4 ≈ 0.705, Tc5 ≈2.183 at Jdx = +1.2 (Jdy = +1.5) (a) and at dx = dy = 6 (b). The temperature dependence of the spontaneous magnetization (c) for the parameters (24) is shown by the red solid line, and the Onsager’s spontaneous magnetization (1) with Tc ≈2.269 is shown by the blue dashed line
